normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def lcsLen (a b : List Int) : Nat := sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the function that returns the length of the intended common subsequence.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lcsLen_spec_upper (a b : List Int) :
  ∀ (s : List Int),
    s.Subseq a → s.Subseq b → s.length ≤ lcsLen a b := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Any integer sequence that is a subsequence of both a and b has length at most the returned value.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lcsLen_spec_lower (a b : List Int) :
  ∃ (s : List Int),
    s.Subseq a ∧ s.Subseq b ∧ lcsLen a b = s.length := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a common subsequence of a and b whose length equals the returned value.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lcsLen_spec_characterization (a b : List Int) :
  lcsLen a b =
    Nat.findGreatest (by
      -- there exists at least one common subsequence (e.g. [])
      have : (List.nil : List Int).Subseq a ∧ (List.nil : List Int).Subseq b := by
        constructor <;> simpa using (List.subseq_nil : (List.nil : List Int).Subseq a)
      exact ⟨0, this.1, this.2⟩) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned length is exactly the maximum length among all common subsequences (i.e., it is the optimum).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lcsLen_nil_left (b : List Int) : lcsLen [] b = 0 := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the first sequence is empty, the longest common subsequence length is 0.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lcsLen_nil_right (a : List Int) : lcsLen a [] = 0 := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the second sequence is empty, the longest common subsequence length is 0.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lcsLen_step_match (a b : List Int) (x : Int) :
  lcsLen (x :: a) (x :: b) = Nat.succ (lcsLen a b) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the heads match, the optimal common subsequence can extend by 1 plus the optimal for the tails.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lcsLen_step_mismatch (a b : List Int) (x y : Int) (h : x ≠ y) :
  lcsLen (x :: a) (y :: b) = Nat.max (lcsLen (x :: a) b) (lcsLen a (y :: b)) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the heads differ, the optimal common subsequence must skip one of the heads, so the result is the max of the two possibilities.
